medical_history.chief_complaint: Acutely swollen tongue, palate, and submandibular region, one to two hours after waking
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Presented with acute angioedema, treated with 100 mg hydrocortisone and 4 mg Piriton, recovered well by the next morning
medical_history.past_medical_history: Right leg below knee amputation due to trauma, benign prostatic hypertrophy, peripheral vascular disease, non-insulin dependant diabetes
medical_history.family_history: No previous personal or family history of angioedema noted

physical_examination.general_examination: Alert, could breathe and swallow but was unable to talk, swelling subsided by the next morning
physical_examination.specialty_examination: No splenomegaly or lymphadenopathy noted during clinical examination by the haematologists
